---
title: "angles_to_rotation_matrix"
generated: true
description: "Convert Euler angles to a rotation matrix (2-D or 3-D)."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.geometry.affine`](../nitrix-geometry-affine.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:angles_to_rotation_matrix -->
```python
angles_to_rotation_matrix(ang: Float[Array, '... a'], *, deg: bool = True) -> Float[Array, '... d d']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:angles_to_rotation_matrix -->
Convert Euler angles to a rotation matrix (2-D or 3-D).

In 2-D a single angle `(..., 1)` yields a `(..., 2, 2)` planar
rotation.  In 3-D three angles `(..., 3)` yield a `(..., 3, 3)`
right-handed intrinsic rotation $R = X\,Y\,Z$ (rotate about the
object's x, then y, then z; the axis order reads with the matrix product
because intrinsic rotations post-multiply -- equivalently the extrinsic
sequence z-then-y-then-x, rightmost factor applied first).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:angles_to_rotation_matrix -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `ang` | `Float` | `...` × `a` | — | Batched Euler angles, with a trailing axis of length 1 (2-D) or 3 (3-D). |
| `deg` | `bool` | — | `True` | If `True` (the default), `ang` is interpreted in degrees; if `False`, in radians. |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `...` × `d` × `d`. Batched rotation matrices, `(..., 2, 2)` in 2-D or `(..., 3, 3)` in 3-D.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:angles_to_rotation_matrix -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `a` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `d` | spatial dimension (2 or 3) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
